# Heratio + AtoM plugin inventory

**Author:** Johan Pieterse, The Archive and Heritage Group (Pty) Ltd
**Date:** 2026-04-28
**Generated by:** `/tmp/plugin-inventory.py` + `/tmp/plugin-md-gen.py` (read-only inspection).

## Verification flag - what `✓` means here

A `✓` in the **Verified** column means this package was exercised end-to-end during this engineering session - not just "composer.json says it exists". Specifically: a request hit it, a query ran through it, an artisan command was registered + run, or its output was checked in a browser/HTTP response.

Everything else is `–` (no claim either way). Most packages are wired correctly - they have a ServiceProvider, routes, views - but "wired" and "verified functional" are different things, and this report doesn't conflate them. Treat `–` as "plausibly works, not proved this session".

**Total:** 94 packages. **Verified this session:** 7.

Verified set is conservative - limited to packages whose live behaviour was inspected during the GenDSs paper run (Discovery, Search, Display, IO Manage, Theme, Core, Settings). The other ~87 packages are wired (ServiceProvider exists, routes load, composer registers them) but were not exercised in this session.

## Caveat

The Heratio packages have a richer wiring surface (ServiceProvider, routes, views, commands, install.sql) so the table is wider on the Heratio side. AtoM plugins follow the older symfony 1.x convention (actions + templates + extension.json + DB row); their checks are coarser by necessity.

**To raise a `–` to a `✓`** for a Heratio package, the package needs an explicit smoke test - typically: hit a route, verify the response, or run an artisan command. Build a CI job that exercises one happy-path per package and tag the corresponding row in this inventory; that would make the Verified column trustworthy at scale.
